items.title: Bessent Says US Will Never Default as Congress Faces Endgame
items.description: (Bloomberg) -- Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the US “is never going to default” as the deadline for increasing the federal debt ceiling gets closer.Most Read from BloombergBillionaire Steve Cohen Wants NY to Expand Taxpayer-Backed FerryWhere the Wild Children’s Museums AreThe Economic Benefits of Paying Workers to MoveNow With Colorful Blocks, Tirana’s Pyramid Represents a Changing AlbaniaNYC Congestion Toll Brings In $216 Million in First Four Months“That is never going to happen,” Bess

items.title: Why Scott Bessent wants to make it easier for banks to own Treasurys
items.description: US regulators are close to easing a key requirement for banks that the Trump administration hopes will inject more liquidity into the Treasury market. The move would mark a retreat from a rule made following the 2008 financial crisis.

items.title: JPMorgan Chief Strategist on Tariffs, Investing Abroad and More
items.description: David Kelly, Chief Global Strategist for J.P. Morgan Asset Management, joins WSJ’s Take On the Week podcast to explain the importance of global investment diversification.

items.title: How The BLSH Portfolio Buys Low And Sells High, Consistently
items.description: The BLSH strategy systematically buys blue-chip stocks at undervalued prices and sells partial positions at overvalued levels. Learn more about the strategy here.
